title: New Belgrade
subtitle: Usce / blocks / Genex
blurb: Contemporary art, Usce Park, and modernist markers across New Belgrade.

microcopy.line: After the old center, the city opens wider.

content.intro:
New Belgrade is best understood as a change of scale. After the fortress and old center, you get broad axes, large buildings, a park by the river confluence, and postwar modernism. The route begins with the museum near Usce, then moves to exterior architectural landmarks.

This is not a route for entering every building. The Palace of Serbia, Sava Center, and Western City Gate are best treated as exterior views unless you have a specific reason to go inside. Use transport between points so the half-day does not turn into a long walk along major roads.
content.routeLogic:
Start with the Museum of Contemporary Art as a clear indoor point.
Usce Park links the museum with the scale of the left bank.
Treat the Palace of Serbia as an exterior landmark.
Sava Center and Genex need transfers, not a long walking link.
content.bestFor:
twentieth-century architecture
a repeat visit
wide-avenue photos
a day with transport
content.notFor:
a first and only day
a walking-only plan
travelers looking for the old city
content.beforeYouGo:
Check Museum of Contemporary Art hours.
Do not assume access to administrative buildings.
Use transport between distant landmarks.
content.timing: Morning works for the museum, while late day gives better light on exterior facades and the broad New Belgrade horizon.
content.fallbackPlan: If the museum is closed, keep Usce Park and two exterior landmarks, then give the extra time to the riverside or fortress.

content.context: The Museum of Contemporary Art stands by Usce Park and opens the left bank well: art inside, and the open space of New Belgrade immediately outside. It starts the route without beginning on a major road.
content.lookFor:
The building form among the trees
The link between museum, park, and rivers
Permanent and temporary exhibitions
A calm move toward open Usce space
content.practical:
Closed on Tuesdays.
Thursday and Saturday hours differ.
Check exhibitions and tickets before going.
content.nextMove: After the museum, step into Usce Park: it is a short move from galleries to the open scale of the district.

stopList.name: Usce Park
stopList.note: Open bank between museum and city

content.context: Usce Park lies near the confluence of the Sava and Danube and gives New Belgrade air after the galleries. Here the route shifts to distances, views, and broad paths that separate the left bank from the old center.
content.lookFor:
Open lawns and long paths
Views toward the fortress across the water
The broad riverside line
Contrast between park and large buildings nearby
content.practical:
Shade may be limited in heat.
After rain, choose firm paths.
Large events can change movement through the park.
content.nextMove: Continue toward the Palace of Serbia or use transport if you do not want to walk beside broad roads.

photo.alt: Фасад Дворца Сербия в Новом Белграде

content.context: The Palace of Serbia matters as an exterior landmark of New Belgrade: a large administrative building and its wide surrounding space show the postwar scale of the district. This route views it from outside.
content.lookFor:
The length of the facade and setback from the street
Axes and open space around the building
The contrast with the dense old center
Perspectives along broad roads nearby
content.practical:
Do not plan to enter without a separate invitation.
Photograph from public areas.
Crossings around the building can take longer than they look.
content.nextMove: It is better to travel to Sava Center, or walk only with good weather and spare time.
content.sourceNote: This is used as an exterior architecture landmark; public entry is not stated here.

content.context: Sava Center shows the business and congress layer of New Belgrade. After reconstruction, the building remains an important modernist landmark, but the route does not depend on entering it.
content.lookFor:
The large volume beside broad roads
Updated facade sections
The link with the business district
Scale that reads better from a distance
content.practical:
Go inside only if there is an event or open area.
Use crossings rather than cutting across major roads.
There is little shade around the building in hot weather.
content.nextMove: The final landmark is farther inside the blocks: travel to the Western City Gate.
content.sourceNote: Sava Center is treated as an exterior landmark; check event schedules separately.

stopList.name: Western City Gate
stopList.note: Genex towers as the final silhouette

content.context: The Western City Gate, known as the Genex towers, gives the route a strong final silhouette. It is an exterior viewing point: the form, bridge between towers, and position among residential blocks are the point.
content.lookFor:
The two towers and top connecting volume
Concrete facade texture
Neighboring New Belgrade residential blocks
How the building works as a city sign on approach
content.practical:
View from public sidewalks.
Do not assume a tourist interior visit.
Check transport or taxi options for the return.
content.nextMove: End by the towers and return by transport; walking back to Usce is too long for this route.
content.sourceNote: Coordinates checked with an open data source; public interior access is not stated.
